description_long: This object is an antique-style cannon mounted on a wooden carriage with four wheels. The cannon barrel is long, cylindrical, and appears to be made of aged, weathered metal with decorative engravings near the breech and muzzle. The carriage is constructed from dark, rough-hewn wood, reinforced with metal bands and bolts, and features two large side wheels and two smaller front wheels for mobility. The rear of the cannon has a knob-like cascabel, and the trunnions are visible, securing the barrel to the carriage. The overall appearance is rustic and historical, with visible signs of wear and patina on both the metal and wood surfaces. This type of object would typically be found in historical urban settings, such as old forts, parks, or as a decorative piece in public squares.
description: A weathered, antique cannon with a long metal barrel mounted on a wooden wheeled carriage, featuring decorative engravings and visible signs of age.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle of the cannon and the symmetrical arrangement of the four wheels on the wooden carriage.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the cannon barrel, the wooden carriage, and the decorative details near the breech.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the cascabel at the end of the barrel and the structure of the carriage's back.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the length of the barrel and the carriage's construction.
